mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a certain threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 4.0% and SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 60% of such triggered instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment with stable prices, encouraging investment and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If periods where CPIAUCSL YoY < 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0% consistently show negative SPX 20d returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, increasing industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving supply-side capacity and demand, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows sustained growth during a recovery period, but SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a significant slowdown in construction and broader economic activity, signaling weakening demand and potentially leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average falls by more than 10% from its 12-month peak
failure_condition: If HOUST experiences a sharp decline as described, but SPX 5d returns are predominantly positive (more than 55% of instances) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and industrial production (INDPRO) growth is stagnant or declining, it suggests stagflationary pressures, leading to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the specified stagflationary conditions are met, but SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, a high GDP growth rate (GDPC1) indicates robust economic activity, leading to increased corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 3.0% during an expansion, but SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances.
